tagline: Premium camping without the price tag.
elevator_pitch: Mountain Mike's is an outfitter franchise located near national parks and popular camping destinations that rents fully stocked RVs, campers, and gear packages so anyone can enjoy a premium outdoor experience without owning expensive equipment. Think of it as a full-service camping concierge that eliminates every barrier between you and the wilderness.
problem: Camping in an RV or camper is one of the best outdoor experiences, but it requires owning a vehicle that costs $20,000 to $50,000 plus a truck capable of towing it. Even if you can afford the gear, RV parks are often crowded and uninspiring, and hauling equipment across the country burns fuel and adds stress. The result: most people who want a great camping trip simply cannot access one.
solution: Mountain Mike's stations a fleet of RVs and campers right outside national parks and top camping destinations. Customers drive up in any vehicle, choose their rig, select a curated gear package (from basic fire and s'mores kits to fully loaded fishing and adventure bundles), and get delivered to a pre-scouted premium campsite. On the way out, hot showers and an ice cream shop cap off the trip.
target_market: Families, friend groups, and couples aged 25 to 55 who love the outdoors but do not own RVs or heavy towing vehicles. Secondary market includes existing RV renters who want a hassle-free, destination-based experience.
revenue_model: Per-trip RV and camper rental fees (ranging from $150 to $400 per night depending on rig size), tiered add-on gear packages ($50 to $200), guided experience upsells (fly fishing tours, hiking guides), and on-site retail including the ice cream shop, hot showers, and camp store. Franchise fees from new locations provide additional revenue.
key_features: Fleet of RVs and campers available on-site near top camping destinations, no towing required
Tiered adventure packages from bare-bones to fully loaded with fishing gear, sleeping bags, and gourmet camp food
Pre-scouted premium campsites with options by the lake, in the trees, or near the mountains
End-of-trip amenities including hot showers and a signature ice cream shop
competitive_advantage: Unlike traditional RV rental companies that require customers to drive rigs across highways, Mountain Mike's positions inventory at the destination, dramatically reducing wear on vehicles and eliminating the need for towing capability. The franchise model allows rapid expansion to high-demand parks, and the curated experience (pre-scouted sites, gear packages, guided tours) creates a premium brand that pure rental companies cannot match.
market_size_estimate: The U.S. outdoor recreation economy generates over $862 billion annually. The RV rental market alone is valued at roughly $1.4 billion and growing 7% year over year. Targeting even 1% of the 48 million U.S. households that go camping annually with an average spend of $300 per trip yields a $144 million addressable market for a franchise network.
go_to_market: Launch a flagship location adjacent to one high-traffic national park (such as Yellowstone or Glacier) with 8 to 12 RVs and campers. Build brand awareness through partnerships with outdoor influencers, Google Ads targeting camping and national park searches, and affiliate deals with travel booking platforms. Validate unit economics over two seasons before offering franchise licenses.
risks: Seasonality could limit revenue to 5 to 7 months per year in many regions, requiring strong peak-season margins to cover off-season costs
Securing permits and land leases near national parks involves complex government approval processes and environmental regulations
High upfront capital for RV fleet acquisition and site development before revenue begins flowing
year_one_milestones: Open flagship location with 10 RVs and achieve 70% occupancy during peak camping season (May through September)
Generate $500K in gross revenue with positive unit economics by end of first full season
Secure letter of intent from 3 franchise candidates for Year 2 expansion
startup_cost_range: $250,000 to $500,000 for a single flagship location including RV fleet, site improvements, gear inventory, and operating capital
